name: #393 · 5f5b0feec536f47a1597556f98eb36368bcf2edf · Karol Wojcik · chore: trigger release v0.5.5

newTests.name: Unit tests for flags-file-path > returns path with session name and .flags.json extension > uses the spel prefix

Integration tests for container element content previews.

   Tests that article, region, listitem, figure, group elements
   get content preview names from innerText. > long content is truncated > content preview is truncated to 80 chars with ellipsis
Tests for eval flags > eval-js -b flag > parses eval-js with --base64 flag

Integration tests for container element content previews.

   Tests that article, region, listitem, figure, group elements
   get content preview names from innerText. > listitem content preview > li element gets content preview
Tests for new global flags > --load-state flag > defaults to nil when no --extension given
Tests for eval command > evaluate JavaScript > parses eval-js with script
Tests for new global flags > --load-state flag > accumulates mixed --extension and --extension= syntax

Integration tests for event listener annotations in snapshots.

   Tests that elements with JS event handlers (onclick, React props, etc.)
   are detected and annotated with [on:click] etc. in the snapshot tree. > inline onclick handler detection > detects onclick handler and annotates with [on:click]
Tests for install command > open --interactive (global flag, sets headed mode) > parses open --interactive
Tests for native.clj parse-global-flags (private) > --cdp flag > parses --cdp <url>
Tests for snapshot command > basic snapshot > parses snapshot --interactive (consumed as global flag)
Tests for native.clj parse-global-flags (private) > --cdp flag > defaults to SPEL_CDP env
Tests for eval flags > eval-js -b flag > parses eval-js with base64 flag
Unit tests for launch flags persistence (write to file + read back) > round-trips flags through file > persists flags and reads them back

Integration tests for container element content previews.

   Tests that article, region, listitem, figure, group elements
   get content preview names from innerText. > article preview uses innerText spacing > content preview has spaces between child block elements

Integration tests for event listener annotations in snapshots.

   Tests that elements with JS event handlers (onclick, React props, etc.)
   are detected and annotated with [on:click] etc. in the snapshot tree. > multiple event types detection > detects multiple handler types on same element

Integration tests for event listener annotations in snapshots.

   Tests that elements with JS event handlers (onclick, React props, etc.)
   are detected and annotated with [on:click] etc. in the snapshot tree. > elements without handlers have no listeners > plain heading has no :listeners in refs

Integration tests for [clickable] annotation noise reduction.

   Tests that cursor:pointer inherited from interactive ancestors
   does NOT mark child elements as [clickable]. > direct cursor:pointer gets [clickable] > element with direct cursor:pointer style is marked clickable

Integration tests for [clickable] annotation noise reduction.

   Tests that cursor:pointer inherited from interactive ancestors
   does NOT mark child elements as [clickable]. > inherited cursor:pointer from link > child element inside <a> link does NOT get separate [clickable]
Unit tests for launch flags persistence (write to file + read back) > read returns empty map for nonexistent session > returns {} when no flags file
Tests for new global flags > --load-state flag > supports --extension=value syntax

Integration tests for container element content previews.

Tests for native.clj parse-global-flags (private) > --profile flag > preserves eval-sci as first command-arg when --profile precedes it
Unit tests for flags-file-path > returns path with session name and .flags.json extension > contains session name and .flags.json
Tests for native.clj parse-global-flags (private) > --cdp flag > combines --profile --cdp for Chrome profile with CDP
Tests for native.clj parse-global-flags (private) > --cdp flag > strips --cdp from command-args
Unit tests for launch flags persistence (write to file + read back) > process-command persists flags to file > writes flags file when _flags present in command
